title: शिव हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शिव हृदयम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीशिवहृदयस्तोत्रस्य
वामदेव ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः।
श्रीसदाशिवो देवता।
ॐ बीजम्। नमः शक्तिः। शिवायेति कीलकम्।
श्रीसदाशिवप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
कर्पूरगौरं करुणावतारं
संसारसारं भुजगेन्द्रहारम्।
सदा वसन्तं हृदयारविन्दे
भवं भवानीसहितं नमामि॥

॥ हृदयम् ॥
हृदयं शम्भोर्ज्ञातव्यं शिवेन कथितं पुरा।
गोपनीयं प्रयत्नेन स्वहृदयमिवेश्वरि॥१॥

शिवो हृदयमस्तीति हृदयं शिव उच्यते।
अतस्तस्य हृदयं ज्ञात्वा शिवो भवति मानवः॥२॥

ॐ शिवाय हृदयाय नमः।
ॐ महेश्वराय शिरसे स्वाहा।
ॐ शम्भवे शिखायै वषट्।
ॐ ईशानाय कवचाय हुम्।
ॐ पशुपतये नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट्।
ॐ रुद्राय अस्त्राय फट्॥३॥

नमः शिवाय सोमाय सगणाय ससूनवे।
सनन्दिने सगङ्गाय सवृषाय नमो नमः॥४॥

यस्मात् परं नापरमस्ति किञ्चिद्
यस्मान्नाणीयो न ज्यायोऽस्ति कश्चित्।
वृक्ष इव स्तब्धो दिवि तिष्ठत्येक-
स्तेनेदं पूर्णं पुरुषेण सर्वम्॥५॥

शिवः शान्तो ह्यमूर्तो यो विश्वस्यास्य परायणम्।
शिवं शान्तमजं भव्यं सत्यं परमकारणम्॥६॥

नमस्ते रुद्ररूपाय नमस्तेऽस्तु पिनाकिने।
नमस्ते पशुनाथाय नमस्ते शूलपाणये॥७॥

शिवोऽहं शिवमात्मानमुपास्ते यः सदा नरः।
तस्य सर्वाणि कार्याणि सिध्यन्ति नात्र संशयः॥८॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
शिवस्य हृदयं दिव्यं यः पठेत् सततं नरः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तः शिवसायुज्यमाप्नुयात्।
मोक्षं च लभते नित्यं शिवध्यानपरायणः॥

॥ इति श्री शिव हृदयं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shiva Hridayam ||

Om Asya Shri Shiva-hridaya-stotrasya
Vamadeva Rishiph. Anushtup Chhandah.
Shri Sadashivo Devata.
Om Bijam. Namah Shaktih. Shivayeti Kilakam.
Shri Sadashiva-prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Karpoora-gauram Karuna-avataram
Samsara-saram Bhujagendra-haram.
Sada Vasantam Hridaya-aravinde
Bhavam Bhavani-sahitam Namami.

|| Hridayam ||
Hridayam Shambhor-jnatavyam Shivena Kathitam Pura.
Gopaniyam Prayatnena Sva-hridayam-iveshvari.||1||

Shivo Hridayam-astiti Hridayam Shiva Uchyate.
Atas-tasya Hridayam Jnatva Shivo Bhavati Manavah.||2||

Om Shivaya Hridayaya Namah.
Om Maheshvaraya Shirase Svaha.
Om Shambhave Shikhayai Vashat.
Om Ishanaya Kavachaya Hum.
Om Pashupataye Netratrayaya Vaushat.
Om Rudraya Astraya Phat.||3||

Namah Shivaya Somaya Saganaya Sasunave.
Sanandine Sagangaya Savrishaya Namo Namah.||4||

Yasmat Param Na-aparam-asti Kinchid
Yasman-naneeyo Na Jyayo-asti Kashchit.
Vriksha Iva Stabdho Divi Tishthaty-eka-
Stenedam Purnam Purushena Sarvam.||5||

Shivah Shanto Hy-amurto Yo Vishvasyasya Parayanam.
Shivam Shantam-ajam Bhavyam Satyam Parama-karanam.||6||

Namaste Rudra-rupaya Namaste-astu Pinakine.
Namaste Pashunathaya Namaste Shulapanaye.||7||

Shivo-aham Shivam-atmanam-upaste Yah Sada Narah.
Tasya Sarvani Karyani Sidhyanti Natra Samshayah.||8||

|| Phalashruti ||
Shivasya Hridayam Divyam Yah Pathet Satatam Narah.
Sarva-papa-vinirmuktah Shiva-sayujyam-apnuyat.
Moksham Cha Labhate Nityam Shiva-dhyana-parayanah.

|| Iti Shri Shiva Hridayam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान: कर्पूर के समान गौर, करुणावतार, संसारसार, भुजगेन्द्रहार, सदा हृदयारविन्द में वसने वाले, भवानीसहित भव (शिव) को नमस्कार।

१. शम्भु का हृदय जानना चाहिए, जो शिव ने पूर्वकाल में कहा था। हे ईश्वरि! इसे अपने हृदय के समान प्रयत्नपूर्वक गोपनीय रखना चाहिए।

२. शिव ही हृदय हैं और हृदय ही शिव कहा जाता है। अतः इसका हृदय जानकर मानव शिव हो जाता है।

३. ॐ शिवाय — हृदय, महेश्वराय — शिर, शम्भवे — शिखा, ईशानाय — कवच, पशुपतये — नेत्रत्रय, रुद्राय — अस्त्र — अंगन्यास।

४. सोम, सगण, ससूनु (पुत्रसहित), सनन्दी, सगंगा, सवृष शिव को बार-बार नमस्कार।

५. जिनसे पर या अपर कुछ नहीं, जिनसे सूक्ष्मतर या बृहत्तर कोई नहीं, जो वृक्ष के समान अडिग दिव्य लोक में एक स्थित हैं — उस पुरुष से यह सब पूर्ण है। (श्वेताश्वतर उपनिषद्)

६. शिव शान्त, अमूर्त, विश्व के परायण, अज, भव्य, सत्य, परमकारण हैं।

७. रुद्ररूप, पिनाकी, पशुनाथ, शूलपाणि को नमस्कार।

८. 'शिवोऽहम्' — शिव आत्मा — ऐसा जो सदा उपासना करता है, उसके सब कार्य सिद्ध होते हैं, इसमें संशय नहीं।

जो सतत इस दिव्य शिवहृदय का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वपापमुक्त होकर शिवसायुज्य प्राप्त करता है। शिवध्यानपरायण को नित्य मोक्ष मिलता है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीशिवहृदयस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य वामदेव ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीसदाशिवो देवता। ॐ बीजम्। नमः शक्तिः। शिवाय कीलकम्। श्रीसदाशिवप्रीत्यर्थे शिवहृदयजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: The heart essence (Hridayam) of Lord Shiva — supreme Shaiva text
Contains the famous Karpoora Gauram dhyanam
Reveals the Shivo'ham truth — 'I am Shiva' realization
Complete Anganyasa with Shiva's six names
Invokes shiva sayujya and liberation from all sins
Includes Shvetashvatara Upanishad verse — authentic Vedic content
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Shiva, Shiva Hridayam (शिव हृदयम्) stands as a heart-essence prayer. This revered text originates from Vamadeva Rishi / Atharvana Upanishad tradition. Devotees recite this prayer to grant Shiva Sayujya and liberation from all sins and reveal the Shivo'ham truth — 'I am Shiva' realization. It is also said to contain the famous Karpoora Gauram dhyanam. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan month. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 7 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Shiva Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shiva Hridayam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. The heart essence of Lord Shiva with Karpoora Gauram dhyanam and Shivoham realization.
relatedSearches: Shiva Hridayam Sanskrit lyrics with meaning
Shiva Hridayam vs Shiva Tandava Stotram

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shiva Hridayam and what makes it unique among Shaiva stotras?
seoFaqs.answer: Shiva Hridayam translates as the Heart of Shiva and is so called because it presents the inner-most secret of Shiva-worship: the truth that Shiva is the very heart of the seeker (Shivo Hridayam-asti). The text is uniquely structured: it includes a viniyoga and dhyana, a complete shadanga-nyasa (six-limb mantra installation) using six Shiva names (Shiva, Maheshvara, Shambhu, Ishana, Pashupati, Rudra), a quoted verse from the Shvetashvatara Upanishad, and the famous Karpura-Gauram dhyana sloka. This combination of tantric nyasa, Vedic upanishad and devotional verses in eight short stanzas is what gives it the name Hridayam, the heart-essence.

seoFaqs.question: Who is the rishi of the Shiva Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: The viniyoga preserved in the text identifies Vamadeva as the rishi, anushtup as the chhand (metre), Sadashiva as the devata, Om as the bija, Namah as the shakti and Shivaya as the kilakam. Vamadeva is one of the great Vedic seers of the Rigveda's fourth mandala and is also one of the five faces of Sadashiva (Vamadeva is the northern face, governing the satvic creation). The attribution to Vamadeva places the hymn within the Shaiva-Vedic stream rather than the purely Tantric Agamic tradition, which is why it is freely recitable by householders without formal tantric diksha.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shivoham realisation mentioned in this hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 8 declares Shivo'ham, I am Shiva. Shivam-atmanam-upaste, the human who constantly worships the Self as Shiva attains accomplishment of all undertakings without doubt. This is the Advaita Shaiva insight: Shiva is not a deity outside the seeker but the very Self (atman) of all beings. The Shivoham contemplation is the same realisation taught in Shankaracharya's Nirvana Shatkam and in the Pratyabhijna school of Kashmir Shaivism. The Hridayam therefore functions both as a stotra of devotional praise and as a contemplative manual for Self-realisation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Karpura Gauram dhyana and why is it included?
seoFaqs.answer: Karpura-Gauram Karuna-avataram is the most universally recited Shiva dhyana sloka, describing Shiva as fair as camphor, an incarnation of compassion, the essence of samsara, garlanded by the king of serpents, ever-residing in the lotus of the heart, joined with Bhavani. Including this verse as the dhyana of the Shiva Hridayam is significant because the sloka itself locates Shiva in the hridaya-aravinda (heart-lotus), perfectly matching the Hridayam theme. The same verse is also chanted at the conclusion of every traditional Hindu funeral and aarti as the universal Shiva-vandana.

seoFaqs.question: What is the role of the six-name nyasa in this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The third verse performs shadanga-nyasa, the installation of six aspects of Shiva on six body parts: Shiva on the heart (hridaya), Maheshwara on the head (shira), Shambhu on the crown-tuft (shikha), Ishana on the protective sheath (kavacha), Pashupati on the three eyes (netra-traya) and Rudra as the protective weapon (astra). This systematic body-installation transforms the recitation from passive prayer into active self-divinisation. By the time the eighth verse arrives at Shivoham, the reciter has literally constructed Shiva on every limb and is prepared to recognise the Self as Shiva. This is why the hymn is held to bestow Shiva-sayujya, identity-with-Shiva liberation.

seoFaqs.question: What does the phalashruti of Shiva Hridayam promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti states that whoever recites the divine Shiva Hridayam continually is freed from all sins, attains Shiva-sayujya (oneness with Shiva), and one who is established in Shiva-dhyana attains moksha eternally. Unlike many Puranic stotras that promise material benefits, the Hridayam frames its phala almost exclusively in terms of moksha and Shiva-sayujya, reflecting its character as a sadhaka's hymn rather than a householder's prayer. It is therefore widely used by Shaiva sannyasis and serious meditation practitioners as part of their daily nitya-karma.
